values.value:
-- TRIGGER HANDLER PATTERN -- ALWAYS use the thin-trigger + handler-class pattern. Never put logic in the trigger body.
Thin trigger example only (delegates 100% to handler): trigger InvoiceTrigger on Invoice (before insert, before update, after insert) { InvoiceHandler.dispatch(Trigger.operationType, Trigger.new, Trigger.old, Trigger.newMap, Trigger.oldMap); }
Handler class shape example only: public class InvoiceHandler { public static void dispatch( System.TriggerOperation op, List<Invoice> newList, List<Invoice> oldList, Map<Id, Invoice> newMap, Map<Id, Invoice> oldMap ) { if (op == System.TriggerOperation.BEFORE_INSERT) handleBeforeInsert(newList); if (op == System.TriggerOperation.BEFORE_UPDATE) handleBeforeUpdate(newList, oldMap); if (op == System.TriggerOperation.AFTER_INSERT) handleAfterInsert(newList, newMap); } }
DO NOT copy this example verbatim. DO NOT emit empty handler methods. DO NOT emit comments like "logic here", "example body omitted", "TODO", or "placeholder". Every generated handler method MUST contain the actual business logic requested by the ticket. Only generate dispatch branches for trigger events required by the ticket and any existing trigger events being preserved.
Naming convention: Trigger name = {ObjectName}Trigger (e.g. InvoiceTrigger, CaseTrigger) Handler name = {ObjectName}Handler (e.g. InvoiceHandler, CaseHandler)
-- ONE TRIGGER PER OBJECT RULE -- Salesforce best practice: ONE trigger per sObject. This system enforces it strictly. The org check always fetches the existing trigger + handler before generation. The result is in orgState under "existingTrigger" and "existingHandlerClass".
STEP ZERO — READ ORG STATE BEFORE WRITING ANYTHING. Check orgState.existingTrigger. This is the ONLY decision point. Ticket wording does NOT override org state.
WARNING — TICKET LANGUAGE IS MISLEADING: Words like "Create a trigger", "Implement a trigger", "Add a trigger" describe the BUSINESS INTENT — NOT an instruction to generate fresh code. If orgState.existingTrigger is not null → you are ALWAYS in MERGE mode.
== MERGE MODE — orgState.existingTrigger IS NOT NULL == STEP 1 — List every event already in the existing trigger. ALL must appear in your output. STEP 2 — List every method in the existing handler class. ALL must appear in your output. STEP 3 — Identify what the ticket is adding (new events or new logic in existing event). STEP 4 — BUILD updated trigger: start from existingTrigger.body verbatim; add new events if any. STEP 5 — BUILD updated handler: start from existingHandlerClass.body verbatim; add new methods or logic. STEP 6 — SELF-CHECK: Does output trigger contain every event from STEP 1? If not, add missing ones. Does output handler contain every method from STEP 2? If not, paste them back in. Is output handler the COMPLETE class — not just a snippet? STEP 7 — Emit the full updated trigger file + meta + handler file + meta.
== CREATE MODE — orgState.existingTrigger IS NULL == No trigger exists. Generate fresh trigger + handler files from scratch. Generate all four files: trigger code, trigger meta, handler code, handler meta.
Trigger handler pattern, one-trigger-per-object rule, merge vs create mode
